name: AI Upscaler Plugin
description: AI-powered video upscaling plugin with Docker microservice architecture. Features automated hardware benchmarking, intelligent fallback system, pre-processing cache, and sidebar integration.
overview: Revolutionary AI upscaling plugin with Docker-based AI processing. Eliminates native DLL conflicts. Features comparison view, TV remote optimization, and sidebar integration.

versions.changelog: v1.5.5.2: Deep scan round 2 — race conditions, SSRF private IP blocking, path traversal hardening, exception info leak fixes, checksum corrections, docker5 tag update.
v1.5.5.0: Critical bug fixes — circuit breaker half-open bypass, ncnn CHW/HWC reshape, audio passthrough (-c:a copy), max_concurrent=0 deadlock, health endpoint accuracy. All 6 Docker images rebuilt.

v1.5.4.4: Docker deep scan — 20+ functional bug fixes (thread safety, circuit breaker, tile blending, ncnn-Vulkan backend, per-model download locks, semaphore capture). All 6 Dockerfiles verified and fixed.
v1.5.4.3: Fix bench buttons — auto-download models on load, forward Docker error codes, consistent API response format. Better download/benchmark UX.
v1.5.4.2: Fix CORS errors in model catalog and performance monitor. Add live video benchmark — test all 35 models against your library content. 3 new proxy endpoints.
v1.5.4.1: Fix multi-frame audio loss, model fallback chain, scale dropdown crash, TensorRT detection, quality 'low' handling. Full settings dashboard, model catalog, live performance monitor.

v1.5.4.0: Multi-Frame VSR (EDVR-M, RealBasicVSR, AnimeSR v2), auto-model selection, image upscaling, priority queue, Prometheus metrics, circuit breaker, webhooks, model fallback chain, 35 AI models. Fixes #46.
v1.5.3.6: 12 new community models — video-optimized (ClearReality, UltraSharp V2, DAT2, HAT, APISR, Compact). Now 30 models total.
v1.5.3.5: RTX 5000 Blackwell (CUDA 12.8) + Apple M5 CoreML support. 6 bugfixes (semaphore crash, memory leak, FFmpeg errors).
v1.5.3.4: New AI models (SPAN, SwinIR, Real-ESRGAN x2+/AnimeVideo), output codec selection, ONNX tiling (OOM fix), 7 critical bugfixes.
v1.5.3.3: Real-time upscaling during playback — two-tier system (WebGL client-side + Server AI). Benchmark-driven tier selection, FPS overlay, auto-fallback.
v1.5.3.2: Fix model selection — correct model is now auto-downloaded, loaded, and used for upscaling. EnsureModelLoaded before every upscale call.

v1.5.3.1: Scheduled task now pre-upscales videos (not just scan), GPU fallback fixes (NVIDIA TensorRT skip, Intel OpenVINO diagnostics), improved /gpu-verify endpoint, player menu redesign with all 14 models.
v1.5.3.0: Fix player button injection (multi-path Docker support, config page bootstrap fallback), add Scheduled Task for library scanning, close stale issues. Fixes #48 #43 #36 #33 #27 #28.

v1.5.2.9: Fix FFmpeg wrapper 404 (dual route fix), fix benchmark 0.0s when Docker AI service unreachable, fix Test Connection cache (fresh check every click), prominent Player Integration section. Fixes #48.
v1.5.2.8: Fix API route mismatch (503 errors on all buttons), config page rebuild (Jellyfin 10.11+), horizontal tabs + collapsible sections, 15+ bug fixes, dark theme. Fixes #39 #36 #48.
v1.5.2.7: Docker fixes (TensorRT/CUDA fallback, Intel OpenVINO GPU, multi-GPU selection), premium sidebar dashboard redesign, player button MutationObserver, health caching, retry logic.
v1.5.2.6: Fixed notification spam, forced English UI, dark theme input fields, XSS security fixes, settings export/import visible, checkbox fix.
v1.5.2.5: Complete English UI, dark theme buttons, bundled dependencies, removed dashboard sidebar link, settings export/import.
v1.5.2.4: Major update - 30+ bug fixes, security hardening, dashboard redesign with tabs, fixed JS components, correct model names, thread safety fixes.
v1.5.2.3: Fixed NotSupported plugin status — lowered targetAbi to 10.11.0.0 for broad Jellyfin 10.11.x compatibility.

v1.5.2.2: Fixed player button not showing (Issue #45) — global script injection via index.html like Intro Skipper. Updated player-integration.js for Jellyfin 10.11+ SPA navigation with viewshow events and correct OSD selectors.
v1.5.2.1: Security hardening (SSH injection fix, path traversal fix, SSH key-only auth), fixed progress tracking, fixed pause/resume, fixed model list sync, dynamic version, build pipeline rewrite.
v1.5.2.0: Intel GPU detection fix (Issue #45), TensorRT fallback fix (Issue #46), fixed model default, full multi-GPU provider chain, version alignment.
v1.5.1.1: SSH configuration hotfix. Fixed SSH fields not saving/loading. Added Test SSH Connection button and backend endpoint.
v1.5.1.0: Real Remote Transcoding (SSH). Implements rffmpeg-style architecture with SSH tunneling, path mapping, and shared transcoding folder support.
v1.5.0.9: Fixed 'selectedModelId is undefined' error prevent models from loading (PR #41). Fixes Issue #42.
v1.5.0.8: Forced English 'Settings saved' message to avoid localization issues.
v1.5.0.7: Fixed 'require is not defined' error when saving settings. Improved UI feedback.
v1.5.0.6: Fixed IP/URL configuration ignoring changes until restart. Now uses dynamic URL resolution on every request.
v1.5.0.5: Compatibility fix for loading spinner (Jellyfin <10.9), Merged PR #40 (Comprehensive Dashboard Fixes including Hardware Status & Connection Check).
v1.5.0.4: Added Test Connection button, English UI text, save success notification.
v1.5.0.3: Fixed Save Configuration button not working (Discussion #38). Added robust event initialization with fallback mechanisms and debug logging.
v1.5.0.2: Fixed settings not saving (#36) - AiServiceUrl now persists correctly
v1.5.0.1: Hotfix - Fixed DI error (#34), Checksum fix (#33), Intel GPU/OpenVINO support (#32)
v1.5.0.0: Docker Microservice Architecture - Eliminates native DLL conflicts. AI processing via external Docker container.
v1.4.9.4: Final Release - Settings Page Fix, Cross-Platform Support, Complete DI Registration.
v1.4.9.3: Verified Service Registration and Settings Polish.
v1.4.9.2: Critical Update. Implemented Real AI Upscaling with ONNX Runtime, Fixed Dependency Injection crashes, Added Model Manager, and Hardware Benchmarking.
